page: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64 with dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 3892.0 GB of RAM, yielding 30.41 GB of memory per core, and includes 14680 GB of local storage. There are no GPUs bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates asymmetric performance characteristics. Single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and bottom 10% for Geekbench single-core and text processing. However, multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is a key strength, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 382.78 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% tier. Redis SET operations are also strong at 80.7 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 55.93 GB/sec.

The qualitative cost profile of the M128m reflects its high resource density, particularly its massive memory capacity and substantial local storage. The primary tradeoff is the low single-core performance and poor single-threaded execution speeds, meaning it is not optimized for workloads reliant on high single-thread clock speeds. Instead, the instance is highly suited for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, caching layers, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores. It features a massive 3892.0 GB of RAM (30.41 GB per core) and 14680 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. While single-core CPU performance is weak, the instance delivers strong multi-core capabilities and top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 382.78 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving. Given its high memory density and substantial local storage, the M128m is qualitatively cost-efficient for massive in-memory workloads but presents a tradeoff in single-threaded efficiency. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and highly parallelized enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 3892 GB RAM, and 14680 GB local storage. It delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance for large-scale databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M128m is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 3892 GB RAM, and 14680 GB local storage, ideal for large-scale databases.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM, substantial local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8280M processors.
3892.0 GB of system memory providing 30.41 GB of RAM per core.
14680 GB of local storage for high-capacity data caching.
Top-tier large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 382.78 GB/sec.
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput Redis operations.
Well-suited for large-scale in-memory databases and caching layers.
